title: Deterministic backtracking can reopen Q2.4's safe learning path
body: Q2.4 established a valid cumulative replay boundary but rejected every full-size candidate after commit 66. Reapplying the same frozen AdamW proposal from the same pre-attempt state at prospectively fixed geometric learning-rate scales may find admissible smaller displacements, allowing quantity learning to continue without weakening the replay or quantity gates.
claims: Q2.4's no-go identified candidate scale, rather than replay authority, as the next controlled variable.
A first-feasible geometric schedule changes no data, metric, threshold, or promotion rule.
